channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australians favour larger vehicles despite record fuel costs
item.description: Australians are continuing to buy larger cars and SUVs even amid record fuel prices, reflecting a mismatch between environmental concerns and consumer behaviour. Despite warnings from experts and grow...

item.title: Challenges Persist in Kenya’s Education System Despite Grade 10 Transition
item.description: Kenya has celebrated the first cohort of Grade 10 students transitioning under the Competency-Based Education (CBE) system, with the Ministry of Education citing a 99 per cent transition rate. However...

item.title: Derek Chisora Faces Deontay Wilder in London with Potential Career Implications
item.description: Tonight, London’s O2 Arena hosts a high-stakes heavyweight bout between Derek Chisora and Deontay Wilder. For Chisora, aged 42, this fight is billed as his final contest, marking the culmination of a ...

item.title: Rare case of a hermaphrodite goat discovered in Geita
item.description: For Geita, Ms Catherine Kachwele, who is a traditional healer, received a goat from a client as partial payment for services after he could not pay in cash. When she brought it home, neighbours and ch...

item.title: Bulls fall just short against Glasgow Warriors in Champions Cup thriller
item.description: The Bulls came close to causing an upset in Glasgow but were narrowly beaten 25-21 by the Warriors in a thrilling Champions Cup clash. The game started with the Bulls dominating the first ten minutes,...

item.title: Lessons from Sydney 2000 to Guide Brisbane 2032 Olympic Preparations
item.description: David Atkins, a key figure behind the Sydney 2000 Olympic ceremonies, has offered advice for Brisbane’s 2032 Olympic plans, emphasising the importance of maintaining ambition during the early stages o...

item.title: French vessels depart to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza
item.description: Several French boats have departed to join an international flotilla aiming to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza. The initiative is part of ongoing efforts to provide relief to residents affected by th...

item.title: Queenfident Masterclass 2026 aims to empower Tanzanian women with confidence and leadership skills
item.description: Dar es Salaam will host the Queenfident Masterclass 2026 on April 11th, designed to empower women in Tanzania to grow their confidence, mindset, and practical skills. The event is aimed at helping wom...

item.title: United States Revokes Immigration Status of Iranian Nationals Linked to Tehran Regime
item.description: The United States government has taken decisive action against several Iranian nationals with ties to the Tehran government by revoking their green cards and visas. This week, Secretary of State Marco...

item.title: U.S. Searches for Missing Pilot After Aircraft Downed in Iran Amid Ongoing Conflict
item.description: The United States military is conducting an intensive search for a pilot missing after an F-15E Strike Eagle was downed over southwestern Iran, marking the first U.S. aircraft loss in Iranian territor...

item.title: Artemis II Mission: Human Crew Travels from Earth to the Moon, Capturing Iconic Images
item.description: On April 2, 2026, the Artemis II mission marked a historic milestone as the first human-crewed journey from Earth to the Moon in the 21st century. The European Service Module (ESM-2) of NASA's Orion s...

item.title: Two People Killed in Shooting at Brampton Elementary Schoolyard
item.description: Two people were tragically killed in a shooting at the St. Joachim Elementary School grounds in Brampton on Friday night, according to Peel Regional Police. The incident occurred around 10:23 p.m., re...

item.title: Pakistan Defence Minister Warns of Action Against Indian False Flag Operations Targeting Kolkata
item.description: On Saturday, Pakistan's Defence Minister Khwaja Asif issued a stark warning to India, stating that any attempt at a false flag operation by India would prompt a response targeting Kolkata. Speaking fr...

item.title: Resident Doctors Begin Nationwide Strike Over Delayed Allowances
item.description: The Nigerian Association of Resident Doctors (NARD) don announce say dem go begin one indefinite strike for all over Nigeria starting from 12:00 a.m. on Tuesday, April 7, 2026. The wahala na because t...

item.title: Kanye West Holds Sold-Out LA Concert Following Apology for Past Antisemitic Remarks
item.description: Kanye West, also known as Ye, returned to the U.S. stage for the first time in nearly five years with a sold-out performance at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles. The concert, attended by 70,000 fans, marke...

item.title: End of Daylight Saving 2026: What Australians Need to Know
item.description: Daylight saving time in Australia is coming to an end for 2026. On Sunday, 5 April at 3am AEDT, clocks will roll back one hour to 2am, giving some Australians an extra hour of sleep. The adjustment is...

item.title: Manila Cathedral to Hold Easter Vigil Led by Cardinal Advincula on Black Saturday 2026
item.description: On Black Saturday, April 4, 2026, the Catholic Church observes one of its most solemn liturgies, the Easter Vigil, commemorating the resurrection of Jesus Christ. This sacred ceremony symbolizes the t...

item.title: IEBC Reports Over 344,000 New Voter Registrations, Nairobi and Kiambu Lead
item.description: The Enhanced Continuous Voter Registration (ECVR) in Kenya has seen a strong start, with over 344,000 Kenyans registering since March 30, 2026. The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEB...

item.title: Trump Evaluates Potential Cabinet Changes Amid Upcoming Midterms
item.description: After a relatively stable period following his return to the White House, President Donald Trump has begun making high-profile Cabinet changes once again. This week, he dismissed Attorney General Pam ...

item.title: Kenya Faces Likely Fuel Price Increase in May Amid Global Oil Market Volatility
item.description: Fuel prices in Kenya are expected to rise in May due to disruptions in global oil supply, particularly linked to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Treasury CS John Mbadi reassured Parliament th...
